# Project Structure

**Purpose:** Quick reference index of what exists in the codebase and where to find detailed documentation.

---

## Documentation Map

- **[ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md)** - Technical decisions and why specific technologies were chosen
- **[PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md)** - How to use established patterns with practical examples
- **[COMPONENTS.md](./COMPONENTS.md)** - Detailed component API reference with props and usage
- **[SERVICES.md](./SERVICES.md)** - Detailed service API reference with methods and examples
- **[BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md)** - Business logic, validation rules, and calculations

---

## Directory Structure

```
client/
├── src/
│   ├── components/          # React components
│   │   ├── form/           # Form components (Au* prefix)
│   │   ├── layouts/        # Layout components
│   │   ├── modal/          # Modal system
│   │   ├── sidebar/        # Sidebar system
│   │   └── svg/            # SVG icon components
│   ├── pages/              # Page components (*Page suffix)
│   │   ├── auth/           # Authentication pages
│   │   ├── transactions/   # Transaction management
│   │   ├── bankAccounts/   # Bank account management
│   │   ├── budgets/        # Budget listing
│   │   ├── budget/         # Single budget view
│   │   └── userCategory/   # Category management
│   ├── services/           # Business logic services
│   ├── repositories/       # Data fetching hooks (use* prefix)
│   ├── shared/             # Shared utilities and types
│   │   ├── store/          # Global state (signals)
│   │   ├── types/          # TypeScript types and enums
│   │   ├── constants/      # App constants
│   │   └── utils/          # Utility functions
│   └── app/                # App initialization and routing
├── tests/                  # E2E tests (Playwright)
├── .claude/                # Multi-agent system configuration
│   ├── docs/              # Documentation for agents
│   ├── agents/            # Agent-specific instructions
│   └── scripts/           # Helper scripts
└── tasks/                 # Agent task outputs
```

---

## Components (`src/components/`)

**See [COMPONENTS.md](./COMPONENTS.md) for detailed API documentation.**

### Form Components (`form/`)

- `AuButton` - Button component with variants and sizes
- `AuField` - Form field wrapper with validation
- `AuSelect` - Select dropdown
- `AuCheckbox` - Checkbox input
- `AuRadioGroup` - Radio button group
- `AuDatePicker` - Date picker
- `AuDropdown` - Dropdown menu
- `AuForm` - Form container
- `AuFormBlock` - Form section wrapper

### Layout Components (`layouts/`)

- `MainLayout` - Main application layout
- `BaseLayout` - Base layout wrapper
- `Menu` - Navigation menu
- `Navigation` - Navigation links
- `LogoutButton` - Logout button

### Modal System (`modal/`)

- `Modal` - Modal dialog component

### Sidebar System (`sidebar/`)

- `Sidebar` - Sidebar container with animations
- `SidebarLayout` - Sidebar layout wrapper

### UI Components

- `PageHeader` - Page header with title and actions
- `Spinner` - Loading spinner
- `EmptyState` - Empty state placeholder
- `CategoryBadge` - Category badge display
- `EditableLabel` - Inline editable label
- `ActionDropdown` - Dropdown menu for actions
- `AIAssistant` - AI chat assistant

### SVG Icons (`svg/`)

- Icon components: `BackSvg`, `CalendarSvg`, `ClearSvg`, `CloseSvg`, `CopySvg`, `EditSvg`, `FileImportSvg`, `FilterSvg`, `HamburgerSvg`, `LogoutSvg`, `PencilSvg`, `PlusSvg`, `TrashSvg`, and more

---

## Services (`src/services/`)

**See [SERVICES.md](./SERVICES.md) for detailed API documentation.**

### Core Services

- `authService` - Authentication and token management
- `modalService` - Modal state management
- `sidebarService` - Sidebar state management with stacking
- `notificationService` - Toast notifications
- `scrollLockService` - Body scroll prevention

### AI Services

- `aiAssistantService` - AI chat backend communication
- `aiConversationStorageService` - AI conversation persistence

### Storage Services

- `localStorageService` - Type-safe localStorage wrapper
- `cookieService` - Type-safe cookie management

---

## Pages (`src/pages/`)

### Authentication

- `AuthPage`, `LoginPage`, `LogoutPage`, `RegistrationPage`, `ProfilePage`

### Transactions (`transactions/`)

- `TransactionsPage` - Main page with list and filters
- `TransactionForm` - Create/edit form
- `TransactionFilter` - Filter component
- `TransactionList` - List display
- Sidebars: Form, Edit, Info, Delete, Bulk, AddCategories

### Bank Accounts (`bankAccounts/`)

- `BankAccountsPage` - Main page
- `BankAccountForm` - Create/edit form
- `BankAccountsList` - List display
- Sidebars: Create, Edit, Info, Delete

### Budgets (`budgets/`)

- `BudgetsPage` - Budget listing
- `BudgetForm` - Create/edit form
- `BudgetPreview` - Budget card
- Sidebars: Create, Edit, Clone, Delete

### Budget (`budget/`)

- `BudgetPage` - Single budget with groups and items
- `BudgetView` - Overview display
- `BudgetInsights` - Analytics
- `BudgetGroup` - Group container (draggable)
- `BudgetItem` - Item display (draggable)
- Group Sidebars: Create, Edit, Delete
- Item Sidebars: Create, Edit, Delete

### User Categories (`userCategory/`)

- `UserCategoryPage` - Category management
- `CategoryForm` - Create/edit form
- Sidebars: Create, Edit, Delete

---

## Repositories (`src/repositories/`)

**Pattern:** Repository hooks wrap API calls with React Query.

**Naming Convention:**

- `useLoad*` - Fetch data (queries)
- `useCreate*` - Create entities (mutations)
- `useEdit*` - Update entities (mutations)
- `useDelete*` - Delete entities (mutations)

### Bank Accounts (`bankAccounts/`)

- CRUD hooks: `useCreateBankAccount`, `useEditBankAccount`, `useDeleteBankAccount`
- Query hooks: `useLoadBankAccount`, `useLoadBankAccountsList`
- Types: `BankAccountDTO`, `CreateBankAccountSDTO`, `EditBankAccountSDTO`

### Budgets (`budgets/`)

- Budget CRUD: `useCreateBudget`, `useEditBudget`, `useDeleteBudget`, `useCloneBudget`
- Budget queries: `useLoadBudget`, `useLoadBudgets`
- Group CRUD: `useCreateBudgetGroup`, `useEditBudgetGroup`, `useDeleteBudgetGroup`
- Group query: `useLoadBudgetGroups`
- Item CRUD: `useCreateBudgetItem`, `useEditBudgetItem`, `useDeleteBudgetItem`
- Item query: `useLoadBudgetItems`
- Types: `BudgetDTO`, `BudgetSDTO`, `BudgetGroupDTO`, `BudgetItemDTO`

### Categories (`category/`)

- CRUD: `useCreateUserCategory`, `useEditUserCategory`, `useDeleteUserCategory`
- Query: `useLoadUserCategoryList`, `useLoadUserBankAccountList`
- Types: `UserCategoryDTO`, `UserCategorySDTO`

### Transactions (`transactions/`)

- CRUD: `useCreateTransaction`, `useEditTransaction`, `useDeleteTransactions` (bulk)
- Queries: `useTransactionList`, `useOnLoadTransaction`, `useTransactionsFromCache`
- Types: `TransactionDTO`, `CreateTransactionSDTO`, `EditTransactionSDTO`

---

## Types and Enums (`src/shared/types/`)

### Core Enums

- `CategoryTypeE` - INCOME, EXPENSE
- `CurrencyE` - Supported currencies
- `GlobalStoreFieldE` - Global store fields
- `TransactionTypeE` - DEPOSIT, WITHDRAWAL
- `TransactionSourceE` - Transaction sources
- `SortOrderE` - ASC, DESC

### API Types (`api/`)

- `ApiGatewayE` - API endpoint definitions
- `FormFieldValidationError` - Validation errors
- `RequestListDataT` - Paginated list response
- `RequestRecordDataT` - Single record response

### Other Types

- `UuidT` - UUID type
- `NotificationT` - Notification object
- `ValidationPatternT` - Validation patterns
- `ReplacePatternT` - Text replacement patterns

---

## Path Aliases

**See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#7-path-aliases) for usage examples.**

Configured in `tsconfig.json` and `vite.config.ts`:

- `Assets/*` → `src/assets/*`
- `Components/*` → `src/components/*`
- `Services/*` → `src/services/*`
- `Repositories/*` → `src/repositories/*`
- `Shared/*` → `src/shared/*`

**Always use path aliases instead of relative imports.**

---

## Key Application Features

### 1. Authentication

Email-based authentication with 6-digit confirmation codes. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#authentication-rules).

### 2. Transaction Management

Create, edit, delete transactions with filtering and bulk operations. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#transaction-rules).

### 3. Bank Accounts

Manage multiple bank accounts with different types and sources.

### 4. Budgeting System

Create budgets with groups and items. Drag-and-drop reordering. Track spending against budget. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#budget-rules).

### 5. Category Management

Create custom income/expense categories. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#category-rules).

### 6. AI Assistant

Chat-based budget assistant with conversation history. See [SERVICES.md](./SERVICES.md#ai-assistant-services).

### 7. State Management

- Global state: @preact/signals-react. See [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md#state-management-architecture).
- Server state: @tanstack/react-query. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#4-data-fetching-pattern).
- Form state: react-hook-form. See [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md#form-state-react-hook-form).

### 8. UI Patterns

- Service-managed modals and sidebars. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#1-modal-pattern).
- Toast notifications. See [SERVICES.md](./SERVICES.md#notificationservice).
- Loading states and empty states.

---

## Development Commands

```bash
yarn dev          # Start dev server
yarn build        # Production build
yarn lint         # Run ESLint
yarn lint:fix     # Fix ESLint issues
yarn test         # Run unit tests
yarn test:watch   # Run tests in watch mode
yarn test:e2e     # Run E2E tests
```

---

## Testing Structure

- **Unit tests:** Adjacent to source files (`Component.test.tsx`)
- **E2E tests:** `tests/` directory (Playwright)
- **Test coverage:** Focus on services, utils, business logic, complex components

See [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md#testing-architecture) for testing strategy.

3. **Check COMPONENTS.md/SERVICES.md** - API reference for existing code
4. **Check BUSINESS_RULES.md** - Understand validation and business logic
5. **Check ARCHITECTURE.md** - Understand technical decisions

Start here, then dive into specific documentation as needed.

1→# Service Documentation 2→ 3→Comprehensive guide to all services in the application. Services manage business logic, state, and side effects. 4→ 5→## Core Services 6→ 7→### authService 8→ 9→**Location:** `src/services/authService.ts` 10→ 11→**Purpose:** Manages user authentication state, token storage, and Sentry user tracking. 12→ 13→**Methods:** 14→ 15→#### `initialize()` 16→ 17→Initializes auth state on app startup by loading token from localStorage. 18→ 19→```typescript 20→authService.initialize(); 21→``` 22→ 23→**Behavior:** 24→ 25→- Reads `ACCESS_TOKEN` from localStorage 26→- If token exists, updates global auth state 27→- Sets Sentry user from token for error tracking 28→ 29→**When to call:** App initialization (in root component or App.tsx) 30→ 31→--- 32→ 33→#### `login(token: string)` 34→ 35→Stores auth token and updates global state after successful login. 36→ 37→```typescript 38→authService.login('eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'); 39→``` 40→ 41→**Behavior:** 42→ 43→- Stores token in localStorage 44→- Updates global `AUTH_TOKEN` state 45→- Sets Sentry user from token 46→ 47→**When to call:** After successful login API response 48→ 49→--- 50→ 51→#### `logout()` 52→ 53→Clears auth state and cached data on logout. 54→ 55→```typescript 56→authService.logout(); 57→``` 58→ 59→**Behavior:** 60→ 61→- Removes token from localStorage 62→- Clears global `AUTH_TOKEN` state 63→- Clears all React Query cache 64→- Clears Sentry user data 65→ 66→**When to call:** User logout, session expiry, or unauthorized errors 67→ 68→--- 69→ 70→**Dependencies:** 71→ 72→- `localStorageService` - Token persistence 73→- `globalStore` - Auth state management 74→- `queryClient` - Cache clearing 75→- `sentryHelpers` - Error tracking 76→ 77→**Usage Example:** 78→ 79→```typescript 80→// On app start 81→useEffect(() => { 82→ authService.initialize(); 83→}, []); 84→ 85→// After login 86→const handleLogin = async (email: string, password: string) => { 87→ const { token } = await loginApi(email, password); 88→ authService.login(token); 89→ navigate('/dashboard'); 90→}; 91→ 92→// On logout 93→const handleLogout = () => { 94→ authService.logout(); 95→ navigate('/login'); 96→}; 97→``` 98→ 99→--- 100→ 101→### modalService 102→ 103→**Location:** `src/services/modalService.ts` 104→ 105→**Purpose:** Manages modal state globally using signals. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#1-modal-pattern) for detailed pattern documentation. 106→ 107→**Methods:** 108→ 109→#### `open(modalName, props?)` 110→ 111→Opens a modal with optional typed props. 112→ 113→```typescript 114→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' }); 115→``` 116→ 117→**Parameters:** 118→ 119→- `modalName` - Modal identifier from `ModalNameE` 120→- `props` - Optional props (type-safe based on modal name) 121→ 122→**Returns:** `modalName` 123→ 124→**Behavior:** 125→ 126→- Adds modal to top of stack 127→- Props are type-checked against `ModalPropsT` 128→ 129→--- 130→ 131→#### `close(modalName: ModalNameE)` 132→ 133→Closes a specific modal. 134→ 135→```typescript 136→modalService.close(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION); 137→``` 138→ 139→**Behavior:** 140→ 141→- Removes modal from global state 142→- Modal component detects removal and closes 143→ 144→--- 145→ 146→**Type Safety:** 147→ 148→```typescript 149→// ModalNameE defines available modals 150→export enum ModalNameE { 151→ TRANSACTION = 'transaction', 152→} 153→ 154→// ModalPropsT defines props for each modal 155→export type ModalPropsT = { 156→ [ModalNameE.TRANSACTION]: { transactionId: UuidT }; 157→}; 158→ 159→// TypeScript enforces correct props 160→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' }); // ✅ 161→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { id: 'uuid' }); // ❌ Type error 162→``` 163→ 164→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 165→ 166→--- 167→ 168→### sidebarService 169→ 170→**Location:** `src/services/sidebarService.ts` 171→ 172→**Purpose:** Manages sidebar state with stacking and graceful animations. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#2-sidebar-pattern) for detailed pattern documentation. 173→ 174→**Methods:** 175→ 176→#### `open(sidebarName, props?)` 177→ 178→Opens sidebar and adds to stack. Removes duplicate if already open. 179→ 180→```typescript 181→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET, { budgetId: 'uuid' }); 182→``` 183→ 184→**Behavior:** 185→ 186→- If sidebar already in stack, removes old instance 187→- Adds new instance to top of stack 188→- Multiple different sidebars can be open simultaneously 189→ 190→--- 191→ 192→#### `openFirst(sidebarName, props?)` 193→ 194→Opens sidebar and clears entire stack first. 195→ 196→```typescript 197→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION); 198→``` 199→ 200→**Behavior:** 201→ 202→- Clears all open sidebars 203→- Opens only the specified sidebar 204→- Use when you want a clean state 205→ 206→--- 207→ 208→#### `close(sidebarName: SidebarNameE)` 209→ 210→Marks sidebar as deleted (triggers close animation). 211→ 212→```typescript 213→sidebarService.close(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); 214→``` 215→ 216→**Behavior:** 217→ 218→- Sets `deleted: true` on sidebar 219→- Sidebar component detects change and plays close animation 220→- After animation, `finalizeClose()` removes from state 221→ 222→--- 223→ 224→#### `closeAll()` 225→ 226→Marks all open sidebars as deleted. 227→ 228→```typescript 229→sidebarService.closeAll(); 230→``` 231→ 232→**Behavior:** 233→ 234→- Sets `deleted: true` on all sidebars 235→- All sidebars animate closed simultaneously 236→ 237→--- 238→ 239→#### `finalizeClose(sidebarName: SidebarNameE)` 240→ 241→Removes sidebar from state after animation completes. 242→ 243→```typescript 244→sidebarService.finalizeClose(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); 245→``` 246→ 247→**Behavior:** 248→ 249→- Removes sidebar from global state 250→- **Called automatically by Sidebar component** after animation ends 251→- **Do not call manually** unless you have a specific reason 252→ 253→--- 254→ 255→**Type Safety:** 256→ 257→```typescript 258→// SidebarNameE defines available sidebars 259→export enum SidebarNameE { 260→ EDIT_BUDGET = 'editBudget', 261→ CREATE_TRANSACTION = 'createTransaction', 262→} 263→ 264→// SidebarPropsT defines props for each sidebar 265→export type SidebarPropsT = { 266→ [SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET]: { budgetId: UuidT }; 267→ [SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION]: void; 268→}; 269→ 270→// TypeScript enforces correct props 271→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET, { budgetId: 'uuid' }); // ✅ 272→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION); // ✅ 273→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); // ❌ Missing required props 274→``` 275→ 276→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 277→ 278→--- 279→ 280→### notificationService 281→ 282→**Location:** `src/services/notificationService.ts` 283→ 284→**Purpose:** Manages toast notifications with auto-dismiss timers. 285→ 286→**Methods:** 287→ 288→#### `addAlert(content: ReactNode, showTime?: number)` 289→ 290→Adds an info notification. 291→ 292→```typescript 293→notificationService.addAlert('Budget saved successfully!'); 294→notificationService.addAlert(
Custom content
, 3000); 295→``` 296→ 297→**Parameters:** 298→ 299→- `content` - Notification message (string or React element) 300→- `showTime` - Duration in milliseconds (default: 5000ms) 301→ 302→**Returns:** `notificationId` (number) 303→ 304→**Behavior:** 305→ 306→- Adds notification to global state 307→- Auto-increments ID 308→- Sets type to `NotificationTypeE.INFO` 309→ 310→--- 311→ 312→#### `removeAlert(id: number)` 313→ 314→Marks notification for removal (starts hide animation). 315→ 316→```typescript 317→notificationService.removeAlert(notificationId); 318→``` 319→ 320→**Behavior:** 321→ 322→- Sets `hideAt` timestamp 323→- Notification component detects and animates out 324→ 325→--- 326→ 327→#### `clearAlert(id: number)` 328→ 329→Marks notification as deleted and cleans up store. 330→ 331→```typescript 332→notificationService.clearAlert(notificationId); 333→``` 334→ 335→**Behavior:** 336→ 337→- Sets `deleted: true` 338→- Calls `clearStore()` to remove all deleted notifications 339→ 340→--- 341→ 342→#### `clearStore()` 343→ 344→Removes all deleted notifications from state. 345→ 346→```typescript 347→notificationService.clearStore(); 348→``` 349→ 350→**Behavior:** 351→ 352→- If no non-deleted notifications remain, clears entire array 353→- Prevents memory leaks from accumulating notifications 354→ 355→--- 356→ 357→#### `addErrorAlert(message: string)` 358→ 359→Logs error to console. 360→ 361→```typescript 362→notificationService.addErrorAlert('Failed to save budget'); 363→``` 364→ 365→**Note:** Currently only logs to console. # Business Analyst Output

## Task ID
task-001-test

## Task Description
Add an error modal that shows API errors to the user.

---

## 1. Requirements Analysis

### 1.1 Current State
The application currently handles API errors using:
- **Toast notifications**: Errors are displayed via `notificationService.addAlert()` which shows temporary toast messages that auto-dismiss
- **Console logging**: `notificationService.addErrorAlert()` only logs errors to the console without user-visible feedback
- **Try-catch pattern**: Components catch errors locally and handle them individually

**Limitations:**
- Toast notifications disappear automatically and may be missed by users
- No persistent error display for critical failures
- No standardized way to show detailed error information
- Users cannot interact with errors (e.g., retry actions, copy error details)

### 1.2 Desired State
Implement a modal-based error display system that:
- Shows API errors in a prominent, persistent modal dialog
- Provides clear error messaging to users
- Allows users to acknowledge and dismiss errors
- Follows existing modal pattern established in the codebase

---

## 2. Scope Definition

### 2.1 In Scope
1. Create a new error modal component following the existing modal pattern
2. Add error modal to the modal name enum (`ModalNameE`)
3. Define typed props for the error modal (`ModalPropsT`)
4. Design error modal UI to display:
   - Error title/heading
   - Error message/description
   - Close/dismiss button
5. Integrate the error modal into the modal system
6. Ensure the modal can be triggered from any component using `modalService.open()`

### 2.2 Out of Scope
- Automatic error interception at axios level (errors should still be caught in components)
- Replacing existing toast notification system
- Error retry mechanisms
- Error logging/tracking to external services
- Form validation error display (already handled by `ValidationError` class)
- Authorization error display (401 already triggers logout)
- Detailed technical error information (stack traces, debug data)
- Multiple error display modes or variants
- Error categorization or severity levels

---

## 3. User Stories

### US-1: Display API Error in Modal
**As a** user
**I want** to see API errors in a modal dialog
**So that** I'm clearly notified when operations fail and understand what went wrong

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Error modal displays when API calls fail
- Modal shows a clear error message
- Modal blocks interaction with the page until dismissed
- Modal can be closed by clicking the close button
- Modal follows the existing modal design pattern

### US-2: Programmatic Error Display
**As a** developer
**I want** to programmatically show error modals from any component
**So that** I can handle API errors consistently across the application

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `modalService.open()` can open the error modal with error message
- Error modal accepts error message as a prop
- Error modal name is defined in `ModalNameE` enum
- Props are type-safe via `ModalPropsT`

---

## 4. Functional Requirements

### FR-1: Error Modal Component
- **Location**: `src/components/modal/` directory
- **Name**: Follow existing modal naming convention
- **Structure**: Use existing `Modal` component wrapper
- **Content**: Display error message passed via props

### FR-2: Modal Integration
- Add new modal name to `ModalNameE` enum
- Define props structure in `ModalPropsT` mapping
- Props should include:
  - `message` (string): The error message to display
  - Optional: `title` (string): Custom error title (default: "Error")

### FR-3: Error Display
- Show error title (default: "Error")
- Show error message clearly
- Provide dismiss/close button
- Follow existing UI patterns from other components

### FR-4: Usage Pattern
Developers should be able to display errors like this:
```typescript
try {
  await someApiCall();
} catch (error) {
  modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, {
    message: (error as Error).message
  });
}
```

---

## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

### NFR-1: Consistency
- Follow existing modal pattern documented in PATTERNS.md
- Use existing `modalService` for state management
- Match existing component structure and naming conventions

### NFR-2: Type Safety
- Fully typed props using TypeScript
- Leverage existing `ModalPropsT` type mapping
- No `any` types

### NFR-3: Accessibility
- Modal should trap focus
- Keyboard navigation support (ESC to close)
- Proper ARIA attributes (inherited from base `Modal` component)

### NFR-4: User Experience
- Clear, readable error messages
- Non-technical language where possible
- Obvious dismiss action
- Consistent with application design language

---

## 6. Integration Points

### 6.1 Existing Systems
- **Modal System** (`src/components/modal/Modal.tsx`): Base modal component to wrap error content
- **Modal Service** (`src/services/modalService.ts`): Service to open/close the modal
- **Modal Enums/Types**:
  - `ModalNameE.ts`: Add error modal identifier
  - `ModalPropsT.ts`: Define error modal props

### 6.2 Usage Locations
The error modal can be used in:
- Repository hooks (data fetching layer)
- Component event handlers (form submissions, button clicks)
- Service methods
- Any location where API calls are made

---

## 7. Assumptions

1. Errors to be displayed are already caught in try-catch blocks
2. Error messages are human-readable (no raw technical errors)
3. The existing modal system supports this use case without modifications
4. A single error modal instance is sufficient (no queuing of multiple errors)
5. Component-level rendering - error modal will be rendered in App.tsx alongside other modals

---

## 8. Dependencies

### 8.1 Existing Components/Services
- `Modal` component (`src/components/modal/Modal.tsx`)
- `modalService` (`src/services/modalService.ts`)
- `ModalNameE` enum
- `ModalPropsT` type
- `AuButton` component for dismiss action

### 8.2 Documentation
- PATTERNS.md - Modal pattern implementation guidelines
- COMPONENTS.md - Modal component API reference
- SERVICES.md - modalService API reference

---

## 9. Success Criteria

The feature is complete when:
1. Error modal component exists and renders correctly
2. Error modal is registered in modal system (enum and types)
3. `modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message })` displays the error
4. Modal can be dismissed by clicking close button
5. Modal follows existing design patterns
6. Type safety is maintained throughout

---

## 10. Open Questions

None. The requirements are clear based on existing documentation and patterns.

---

- **Project Structure**: `.claude/docs/PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md`
- **Modal Pattern**: `.claude/docs/PATTERNS.md#1-modal-pattern`
- **Component Documentation**: `.claude/docs/COMPONENTS.md#modal-system`
- **Service Documentation**: `.claude/docs/SERVICES.md#modalservice`
- **Existing Modal**: `src/components/modal/Modal.tsx`
- **Modal Service**: `src/services/modalService.ts`

## 1. Requirements Analysis

### 1.1 Current State
The application currently handles API errors using:
- **Toast notifications**: Errors are displayed via `notificationService.addAlert()` which shows temporary toast messages that auto-dismiss
- **Console logging**: `notificationService.addErrorAlert()` only logs errors to the console without user-visible feedback
- **Try-catch pattern**: Components catch errors locally and handle them individually

**Limitations:**
- Toast notifications disappear automatically and may be missed by users
- No persistent error display for critical failures
- No standardized way to show detailed error information
- Users cannot interact with errors (e.g., retry actions, copy error details)

### 1.2 Desired State
Implement a modal-based error display system that:
- Shows API errors in a prominent, persistent modal dialog
- Provides clear error messaging to users
- Allows users to acknowledge and dismiss errors
- Follows existing modal pattern established in the codebase

---

## 2. Scope Definition

### 2.1 In Scope
1. Create a new error modal component following the existing modal pattern
2. Add error modal to the modal name enum (`ModalNameE`)
3. Define typed props for the error modal (`ModalPropsT`)
4. Design error modal UI to display:
   - Error title/heading
   - Error message/description
   - Close/dismiss button
5. Integrate the error modal into the modal system
6. Ensure the modal can be triggered from any component using `modalService.open()`

### 2.2 Out of Scope
- Automatic error interception at axios level (errors should still be caught in components)
- Replacing existing toast notification system
- Error retry mechanisms
- Error logging/tracking to external services
- Form validation error display (already handled by `ValidationError` class)
- Authorization error display (401 already triggers logout)
- Detailed technical error information (stack traces, debug data)
- Multiple error display modes or variants
- Error categorization or severity levels

---

## 3. User Stories

### US-1: Display API Error in Modal
**As a** user
**I want** to see API errors in a modal dialog
**So that** I'm clearly notified when operations fail and understand what went wrong

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Error modal displays when API calls fail
- Modal shows a clear error message
- Modal blocks interaction with the page until dismissed
- Modal can be closed by clicking the close button
- Modal follows the existing modal design pattern

### US-2: Programmatic Error Display
**As a** developer
**I want** to programmatically show error modals from any component
**So that** I can handle API errors consistently across the application

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `modalService.open()` can open the error modal with error message
- Error modal accepts error message as a prop
- Error modal name is defined in `ModalNameE` enum
- Props are type-safe via `ModalPropsT`

---

## 4. Functional Requirements

### FR-1: Error Modal Component
- **Location**: `src/components/modal/` directory
- **Name**: Follow existing modal naming convention
- **Structure**: Use existing `Modal` component wrapper
- **Content**: Display error message passed via props

### FR-2: Modal Integration
- Add new modal name to `ModalNameE` enum
- Define props structure in `ModalPropsT` mapping
- Props should include:
  - `message` (string): The error message to display
  - Optional: `title` (string): Custom error title (default: "Error")

### FR-3: Error Display
- Show error title (default: "Error")
- Show error message clearly
- Provide dismiss/close button
- Follow existing UI patterns from other components

### FR-4: Usage Pattern
Developers should be able to display errors like this:
```typescript
try {
  await someApiCall();
} catch (error) {
  modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, {
    message: (error as Error).message
  });
}
```

---

## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

### NFR-1: Consistency
- Follow existing modal pattern documented in PATTERNS.md
- Use existing `modalService` for state management
- Match existing component structure and naming conventions

### NFR-2: Type Safety
- Fully typed props using TypeScript
- Leverage existing `ModalPropsT` type mapping
- No `any` types

### NFR-3: Accessibility
- Modal should trap focus
- Keyboard navigation support (ESC to close)
- Proper ARIA attributes (inherited from base `Modal` component)

### NFR-4: User Experience
- Clear, readable error messages
- Non-technical language where possible
- Obvious dismiss action
- Consistent with application design language

---

## 6. Integration Points

### 6.1 Existing Systems
- **Modal System** (`src/components/modal/Modal.tsx`): Base modal component to wrap error content
- **Modal Service** (`src/services/modalService.ts`): Service to open/close the modal
- **Modal Enums/Types**:
  - `ModalNameE.ts`: Add error modal identifier
  - `ModalPropsT.ts`: Define error modal props

### 6.2 Usage Locations
The error modal can be used in:
- Repository hooks (data fetching layer)
- Component event handlers (form submissions, button clicks)
- Service methods
- Any location where API calls are made

---

## 7. Assumptions

1. Errors to be displayed are already caught in try-catch blocks
2. Error messages are human-readable (no raw technical errors)
3. The existing modal system supports this use case without modifications
4. A single error modal instance is sufficient (no queuing of multiple errors)
5. Component-level rendering - error modal will be rendered in App.tsx alongside other modals

## 9. Success Criteria

The feature is complete when:
1. Error modal component exists and renders correctly
2. Error modal is registered in modal system (enum and types)
3. `modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message })` displays the error
4. Modal can be dismissed by clicking close button
5. Modal follows existing design patterns
6. Type safety is maintained throughout
